design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore world, manage survival mechanics (eating, sleeping), combat monsters via alignment system, gain experience and equipment
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral choice system (Justice/Normal/Evil) that mechanically gates progression and content, balanced against survival resource management (hunger, fatigue, inventory weight)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a chosen hero investigating a catastrophe, with personal agency over moral alignment affecting gameplay consequences
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hardcore simulation-first design prioritizing realism over convenience: mandatory eating/sleeping, encumbrance weight system, day-night cycles, moral fiber meter directly gating content. Game demands player commitment to systems rather than hand-holding. Four distinct character classes force meaningfully different playstyles.

difficulty.checkpointing: Four save slots available; player must manage saves across different character playthroughs
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moral alignment system creates emergent difficulty: evil path spawns frequent traps if MF drops to zero; good path unlocks item rewards. Character class selection significantly impacts challenge. Encumbrance and survival mechanics add resource tension.

progression.upgrade_system: Experience points from combat, equipment drops from defeated monsters, spells learned, character class determines stat growth paths
progression.unlock_mechanism: Moral alignment gates different monster types and environmental content. High moral fiber unlocks item rewards in world; low moral fiber triggers trap mechanisms. Equipment and spells unlock through monster defeats and world exploration.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct character classes with different playstyles
Moral alignment (Justice/Normal/Evil) creates three distinct campaign experiences
Multiple save slots encourage different moral paths
Day-night cycle and survival mechanics vary based on playstyle

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene establishes explosion and monster emergence
Character classes provide role-based narrative framing
Moral fiber system creates player-driven narrative through alignment choices
Ending cutscene resolves chosen hero's quest
In-world clock and survival mechanics reinforce immersion and world persistence

faction_indicators: Justice alignment (high moral fiber) - reward-oriented, good monster alliance
Evil alignment (low moral fiber) - chaos-oriented, trap mechanisms, evil monster interactions
danger_signals: Dropping moral fiber below zero triggers frequent traps across the world
Failing to eat or sleep regularly decreases HP and attack power
Carrying over load capacity reduces movement speed significantly
environmental_storytelling: Fairyland becomes corrupted by monster invasion after initial explosion near City of the Woods
Trap density reflects player's moral choices (high traps = low morality)
Random item locations appear in world based on moral alignment
NPC towns provide services (banking, eating, sleeping) reinforcing settlement importance
